title: [Synth] Add FunctionalReduction pass (w/o SAT solving)
body:
This pass performs functional reduction for single-bit `synth` logic. It runs random-pattern simulation to build candidate equivalence classes for `i1` values with matching signatures, then uses a SAT solver to verify each candidate pair before materializing the proven equivalences with `synth.choice` operations.

See "FRAIGs: A Unifying Representation for Logic Synthesis and Verification, Tech. Rep., 2005" by A. Mishchenko et al. for details on the underlying algorithm. https://people.eecs.berkeley.edu/~alanmi/publications/2005/tech05_fraigs.pdf

To split the PR, equivalence check based on SAT solving is currently omitted. Instead it uses test-only attribute as an oracle of equivalence. SAT solving or other enhancements (equivalence class refinement based on CEX, canonicalization of inversion) will be added in a follow-up.
